phase1.explanation:
Enums define a fixed set of constants:

Basic enum:
• enum Day { MONDAY, TUESDAY, ... }
• Values are constants (uppercase by convention)

Key features:
• Type-safe — compiler checks values
• Can be used in switch statements

Useful methods:
• values() — returns array of all values
• ordinal() — position (0-based index)
• valueOf(String) — parses string to enum

Advanced enums:
• Can have fields and constructors
• Can have methods
• Enum constants can have different data

When to use enums:
• Fixed set of values (days, months, status)
• Error codes
• Configuration options

⚠️ Enums are classes — they can't extend anything else!

phase2.errorMessage:
Test.java:9: error: constant string required
            case Red:  // Wrong case — should be RED
                 ^
1 error
phase2.explanation:
When using enums in switch, you must use the EXACT constant name (case-sensitive).

Color.RED is the constant, but you wrote Red.

FIX: Use RED, not Red.

In switch statements, you can use the enum constant name without the enum prefix:
switch (color) {
    case RED:     // ✓ correct
    case GREEN:   // ✓ correct
}

phase3.prompt:
Create an enum OrderStatus with values:
• NEW, PROCESSING, SHIPPED, DELIVERED, CANCELLED

Add methods:
• isActive() — returns true for NEW, PROCESSING, SHIPPED (not delivered or cancelled)
• getDescription() — returns a description for each status

In main, iterate through all statuses and print their description and active status.
dialogueHints: Add a constructor to store description if needed
isActive() returns boolean based on current status
